description: Conga + PROS ICP Scoring Engine — structured weighted scoring data. Edit industry_matrix and signal_taxonomy here; prompts in index.html reference this file.

signal_schema.in_flight: true = signal only discoverable during active deal (discovery call, demo, proposal). false = findable pre-call via web search / tools.
signal_schema.negative: true = signal reduces score (competitive risk or displacement barrier). false = positive signal.
signal_schema.discovery_method: Where / how to find this signal pre-call (if in_flight=false) or how to surface it in-call (if in_flight=true).

scoring_weights.description: Weights must sum to 1.0. Adjust here to shift priority across dimensions.

recency_decay.description: Multiplier applied to a signal's base_score based on how recently it occurred. Signal score = base_score × multiplier. 2-year hard cutoff.

signal_taxonomy.signal: Publicly announced transformation initiative
signal_taxonomy.discovery_method: Google News, company press releases, LinkedIn posts, company blog — search '[company] transformation initiative'
signal_taxonomy.notes: Any iteration of 'transformation' — digital, operational, commercial, business

signal_taxonomy.signal: CRM or ERP migration or consolidation project
signal_taxonomy.discovery_method: LinkedIn job postings ('SAP S/4HANA migration', 'Salesforce implementation'), press releases, trade press
signal_taxonomy.notes: SAP S/4HANA migration, Salesforce implementation, Oracle consolidation, etc.

signal_taxonomy.signal: Recent FDA product pre-approval
signal_taxonomy.discovery_method: FDA.gov PDUFA dates, BioPharmaDive, FiercePharma, company press releases
signal_taxonomy.notes: ALWAYS flag this account for CLM regardless of product selected. Pre-approval drives compliance docs, submission workflows, contract complexity.

signal_taxonomy.signal: Recent M&A or funding round
signal_taxonomy.discovery_method: Crunchbase, PitchBook, PEHub, company press releases, SEC EDGAR (8-K filings)
signal_taxonomy.notes: Acquisition, merger, PE backing, Series C+, growth equity. Post-M&A = contract standardization need.

signal_taxonomy.signal: Recent FDA product approval
signal_taxonomy.discovery_method: FDA.gov approval database, company press releases, BioPharmaDive
signal_taxonomy.notes: Post-approval commercial launch creates contract, document, and distribution complexity.

signal_taxonomy.signal: New operating division or business unit announced
signal_taxonomy.discovery_method: Press releases, SEC 10-K/8-K ('new operating segment'), investor day presentations, PE portfolio news — mean ACV $57,776 for these deals
signal_taxonomy.notes: Existing customers adding a new division generate the highest mean ACV of any deal type. Also relevant for cold prospects restructuring.

signal_taxonomy.signal: Recent hiring spree (10%+ headcount growth in 1 year)
signal_taxonomy.discovery_method: LinkedIn headcount trend (company page → People tab), Crunchbase headcount history
signal_taxonomy.notes: Rapid headcount growth signals scale pressure on manual processes.

signal_taxonomy.signal: Recent C-suite hire
signal_taxonomy.discovery_method: LinkedIn (filter by start date), press releases, company blog, news search '[company] appoints CEO/CFO/CRO'
signal_taxonomy.notes: New CEO, CFO, CRO, CLO, CPO, CPTO — new leaders often mandate tooling change in first 90 days.

signal_taxonomy.signal: News citing revenue leakage, contract dispute, missed renewal, or billing error
signal_taxonomy.discovery_method: Google News, Glassdoor reviews, LinkedIn posts, SEC risk factor language in 10-K
signal_taxonomy.notes: Explicit public pain signal. Highest buying urgency indicator.

signal_taxonomy.signal: Existing Conga Composer customer
signal_taxonomy.discovery_method: Internal Conga CRM — filter existing accounts by Composer product. Primary cross-sell setup for CLM/DocAuto.
signal_taxonomy.notes: 3,712 of 5,789 won deals trace back to Composer. Existing Composer customers are the most fertile ground for CLM/DocAuto cross-sell. Do not prospect cold for CPQ/DocAuto without checking this first.

signal_taxonomy.signal: Infor M3 or NetSuite ERP confirmed
signal_taxonomy.discovery_method: LinkedIn job postings ('Infor M3 experience', 'NetSuite ERP'), BuiltWith, Stackshare, G2 tech stack profiles
signal_taxonomy.notes: PROS win rates: Infor M3 = 62.5%, NetSuite = 57.1% — highest ERP win rates for POM, better than SAP S/4HANA (53.6%).

signal_taxonomy.signal: New relevant VP hire (product-specific)
signal_taxonomy.discovery_method: LinkedIn (search by title + company, filter by start date < 6 months). See hiring_signals map per product.
signal_taxonomy.notes: Use hiring_signals map per product to determine relevance.

signal_taxonomy.signal: News citing growth, new markets, expansion, or rapid scale
signal_taxonomy.discovery_method: Google News, company press releases, LinkedIn company posts, trade press

signal_taxonomy.signal: Earnings call or 10-K citing digital transformation, margin expansion, customer experience, or operational efficiency
signal_taxonomy.discovery_method: SEC EDGAR (10-K, earnings call transcripts), Seeking Alpha, company IR page, Motley Fool transcripts
signal_taxonomy.notes: Public company signal; implies strategic budget exists.

signal_taxonomy.signal: High revenue or employee count growth verified (20%+ YoY)
signal_taxonomy.discovery_method: Crunchbase, LinkedIn headcount trend, D&B Hoovers, company annual report
signal_taxonomy.notes: Growth indicator; part of growth_indicators dimension.

signal_taxonomy.signal: Verified Salesforce CRM usage
signal_taxonomy.discovery_method: BuiltWith, HG Insights, LinkedIn job postings ('Salesforce administrator', 'SFDC'), G2 stack profile
signal_taxonomy.notes: Highest-fit integration path for all Conga products. Confirmed via BuiltWith, HG Insights, or LinkedIn.

signal_taxonomy.signal: Glassdoor reviews mentioning contracts are a mess, quoting is manual, no deal desk, or legal slows everything down
signal_taxonomy.discovery_method: Glassdoor.com — search company, read Sales/RevOps/Legal reviews, filter by keywords
signal_taxonomy.notes: Internal pain signal sourced from employee sentiment.

signal_taxonomy.signal: LinkedIn post from Sales Ops or Legal Ops leader discussing process pain or tooling gaps
signal_taxonomy.discovery_method: LinkedIn search: '[company name] Sales Operations OR Legal Operations', filter by Posts in past 90 days

signal_taxonomy.signal: DocuSign or Adobe Sign contract renewal within 6 months
signal_taxonomy.discovery_method: Ask in first discovery call: 'When does your current eSign or document contract renew?' Cannot be confirmed pre-call.
signal_taxonomy.notes: Time-bound displacement window. Highest urgency signal for DocAuto. Score as 9 if renewal < 6 months, 7 if 6–12 months.

signal_taxonomy.signal: Contract volume or document volume disclosed by prospect
signal_taxonomy.discovery_method: Ask in discovery: 'How many contracts do you execute per year?' or 'What's your monthly document/eSign volume?' Maps to business_model_thresholds.
signal_taxonomy.notes: High propensity: 1,000+ contracts/year (CLM) or 5,000+ docs/month (DocAuto). Medium: 500+ contracts/year.

signal_taxonomy.signal: Quote volume or pricing complexity disclosed by prospect
signal_taxonomy.discovery_method: Ask in discovery: 'How many quotes does your team generate per month?' or 'How many SKUs are in your pricing catalog?' Maps to business_model_thresholds.
signal_taxonomy.notes: High propensity: 250+ quotes/month (CPQ) or 10,000+ SKUs/legacy quotes (POM).

signal_taxonomy.signal: Champion confirmed with active pain and executive access
signal_taxonomy.discovery_method: Validate in discovery/demo: champion can articulate business pain, has access to economic buyer, and is actively selling internally.
signal_taxonomy.notes: 48% of all losses are No Decision — a confirmed champion is the single biggest deal protection signal.

signal_taxonomy.signal: Multiple revenue streams disclosed (subscription + transactional + services)
signal_taxonomy.discovery_method: Ask in discovery: 'How do you bill customers — subscription, usage-based, one-time?' RLM requires genuine billing complexity.
signal_taxonomy.notes: Hard prerequisite for RLM. Without multiple revenue streams, do not pursue.

signal_taxonomy.signal: SAP CRM confirmed as primary CRM (not SAP ERP)
signal_taxonomy.discovery_method: LinkedIn job postings ('SAP CRM', 'SAP Sales Cloud'), BuiltWith — distinguish from SAP ERP/S4HANA which is a positive signal.
signal_taxonomy.notes: NEGATIVE SIGNAL. SAP CRM users are locked into SAP's native pricing tooling. PROS win rate with SAP CRM = 25.6% vs. 53.6% for SAP ERP. ERP = positive, CRM = negative for POM.

signal_taxonomy.signal: PDF Butler confirmed in Salesforce AppExchange stack
signal_taxonomy.discovery_method: Cannot confirm pre-call — ask in discovery: 'What Salesforce AppExchange apps do you use for document generation?' or 'Are you using PDF Butler today?'
signal_taxonomy.notes: NEGATIVE SIGNAL. PDF Butler head-to-head win rate = 16%. Hard displacement. Flag immediately if discovered — reassess deal viability.

signal_taxonomy.signal: Job postings in relevant fields per product hiring map
signal_taxonomy.discovery_method: LinkedIn Jobs, Indeed, company careers page — filter by job titles in hiring_signals map
signal_taxonomy.notes: Score 5 base; elevate to 7 if multiple openings in same function.

signal_taxonomy.signal: Rapid international expansion — new country offices or foreign entity filings
signal_taxonomy.discovery_method: Press releases, LinkedIn company posts, D&B foreign entity filings, company website (new office announcements)

signal_taxonomy.signal: Conference speaker or panelist on contract automation, scaling revenue ops, or CLM strategy
signal_taxonomy.discovery_method: Conference agendas (Dreamforce, CLOC, Gartner, Revenue Summit), LinkedIn activity search

signal_taxonomy.signal: LinkedIn company page shows Legal, Finance, and Sales all listed as large departments
signal_taxonomy.discovery_method: LinkedIn company page → People tab → filter by department
signal_taxonomy.notes: Signals contract complexity exists across multiple functions.

Hospital Systems.description: General and surgical hospitals — procurement-driven, Epic/Oracle stack, rarely buy

HLS - Pharma & Biotech.description: Pharmaceutical manufacturing, biotech, biotech R&D — top sub-vertical across all products
HLS - Pharma & Biotech.notes: FDA signals amplify CLM/DocAuto/Aime scores. Sub-verticals: Pharmaceutical Manufacturing, Biotech, Biotech R&D, Generic Pharmaceuticals, Life Sciences R&D.

HLS - Medical Device.description: Medical device manufacturers and surgical equipment
HLS - Medical Device.notes: High contract complexity, compliance docs, regulatory submissions. Sub-verticals: Surgical and Medical Devices, General Healthcare Equipment.

HLS - Payers & Health Insurance.description: Health insurers, managed care organizations, payers
HLS - Payers & Health Insurance.notes: High document volume and compliance requirements. CPQ/POM weak fit.

HLS - Healthcare Services & SaaS.description: Healthcare IT/SaaS companies, healthcare services providers — NOT hospital systems
HLS - Healthcare Services & SaaS.notes: SaaS-like billing complexity. Better fit than hospital systems. Sub-verticals: Health Care Management Software.

SaaS / Technology.description: Pure software and SaaS companies. High fit across all products.
SaaS / Technology.notes: Subscription + usage billing = RLM fit. Complex contracts = CLM. Do NOT apply these scores to IT Services & Consulting sub-vertical — use that row instead.

IT Services & Consulting.description: IT consulting firms, managed services, system integrators — separate from SaaS
IT Services & Consulting.notes: Worst closed-lost rate across all products (4–8%). Services model = low fit. Consistently top loss sub-vertical. Do not conflate with SaaS/Technology.

FinServ - Banking & Investment.description: Commercial banks, investment banks, private equity, asset management
FinServ - Banking & Investment.notes: Investment banking is a top DocGen sub-vertical — active market. CPQ/POM weak fit.

FinServ - Insurance.description: P&C, life, health insurance carriers and brokers
FinServ - Insurance.notes: High document volume and compliance requirements. DocAuto strongest fit. High volume services companies (HomeServe-type) also qualify here.

Manufacturing - General.notes: POM score reduced from 8 to 7: new logo win rate 24.8% with 3 active competitors (PriceFx, Vendavo, Zilliant). CPQ/POM strong but competitive. Check sub-vertical — Pharma/Med Device manufacturing should use HLS scores.

Manufacturing - Chemical & Industrial.description: Chemical manufacturers, industrial goods, electrical & automotive parts
Manufacturing - Chemical & Industrial.notes: High POM/CPQ per win rate data and user guidance. POM chemical win rate = 17.5% (competitive but market exists).

Wholesale & Distribution.notes: Core distribution motion for POM. Industrial Machinery Distribution is a top CPQ closed-lost sub-vertical = active market.

Rental Services & Equipment.description: Equipment rental, modular space rental, specialty rental — distinct from logistics
Rental Services & Equipment.notes: PROS new logo win rate = 60%, mean ACV $371K — highest-performing POM vertical. Previously missing from matrix. Royal Automobile Association (Transport) = $675K RLM deal. Prioritize for POM outreach.

Building Materials & Construction.description: Building materials manufacturers, construction companies, contractors
Building Materials & Construction.notes: High CPQ/POM per user guidance. Low CLM/DocAuto.

Food & Beverage / Consumer Goods.notes: High POM ACV ($389K) but low win rate (21.6%) — market exists but highly competitive. Score reflects opportunity; rep should validate champion strength before investing heavily.

Cargo & Logistics.description: Freight, shipping, logistics, supply chain services
Cargo & Logistics.notes: CLM score raised from 4 to 6: won deals average $25,392 ACV — 3rd highest across all industries. High-value, hard-to-win vertical; prioritize when warm signal exists. Airlines = 42.4% POM win rate.

Telecom.description: Telecommunications carriers, equipment providers, internet services
Telecom.notes: Complex billing (subscription + transactional + usage) = strong RLM fit. Mean ACV $26K is 2nd highest but bimodal — most deals are small Composer; CLM wins are rare but large. Prioritize RLM.

Energy / Oil & Gas.description: Oil and gas producers, energy utilities, renewables
Energy / Oil & Gas.notes: POM = 7.1% win rate — avoid unless warm referral. CLM/DocAuto moderate for regulatory compliance docs.

Professional Services.description: Management consulting, legal services, accounting firms — NOT IT consulting
Professional Services.notes: DocGen strongest here per closed-lost data. Home warranty / disaster remediation services = DocAuto 9. Management consulting = low fit overall.

Retail.notes: Retail pricing = POM market exists. Otherwise low fit. Pure B2C/DTC with 10,000+ docs/month can qualify for DocAuto.

Education & Nonprofit.notes: DocGen/eSign has Education presence. Otherwise very low fit. Exception: existing CLM champion in Education can close large ($121K mean ACV) — do not auto-disqualify warm deals.

Government.description: Federal, state, and local government agencies
Government.notes: Highest loss rate in existing ICP. Procurement constraints. Avoid unless existing relationship. 55.7% win rate belies low ACV ceiling ($9,979 mean).

sub_vertical_overrides.notes: A SaaS company serving healthcare is better scored as SaaS than HLS.
High doc volume service company — DocAuto score overrides Professional Services row.
Do NOT use SaaS / Technology scores. This sub-vertical is the worst closed-lost performer across all products.
PROS 60% new logo win rate. Do not score as Cargo & Logistics or IT Services.

hiring_signals.description: Job titles that indicate buying intent by product. Match against LinkedIn job postings and recent hires.

business_model_thresholds.description: Volume thresholds that indicate high fit for each product. Use these to score the business_model_fit dimension.

DocAuto.notes: Salesforce CRM is a hard requirement for high score. Without Salesforce, cap at 6.

POM.high_propensity: 10,000+ legacy quotes for historical pricing context; or 10,000+ SKUs

Aime.high_propensity: Large existing contract repository (10,000+ contracts); existing CLM or CLM in evaluation
Aime.notes: Not a greenfield product — requires existing contract volume.

RLM.high_propensity: Multiple revenue streams: subscription + transactional + professional services billing

strategic_alignment_initiatives.description: If a company's announced strategy matches any of these, score strategic_alignment as Y (1.0 multiplier). If unclear, score as ? (0.5 multiplier). If none, N (0 multiplier).
initiatives: Compliance mandate (FDA, SOX, GDPR, HIPAA, etc.)
Pricing modernization or margin expansion initiative
Contract standardization or legal operations transformation
Operational efficiency or cost reduction program
Subscription or recurring revenue model shift

salesforce_industry_map._description: Maps broad Salesforce CRM industry labels to the correct matrix key. 'requires_subclassification' means Claude must identify the sub-type before scoring.

Healthcare and Life Sciences.note: Salesforce rolls hospital systems, pharma, biotech, medtech, payers, and healthcare SaaS into one label. Claude MUST sub-classify each account.

product_notes.CLM: Primary motion: Enterprise and Commercial, $50M+ revenue minimum. Pharma/Biotech/MedDevice + Salesforce = highest scores (9–10). Not a new logo product below $50M. Existing Composer customers are the primary cross-sell source.
product_notes.CPQ: Pure enterprise product, $250M+ minimum. REQUIRES existing Conga relationship (CLM or Composer) as near-hard gate — only 7 new logo wins in 4 years. 23.7% Not Qualified rate. Do not prospect cold for CPQ.
product_notes.POM: New logo close rate 29.8%. Vertical-based scoring overrides revenue tier. Oil & Gas 7.1% win rate — avoid. Rental Services 60% win rate — prioritize. EMEA win rate 53.9% vs. 47.1% AMER — apply geo bonus for EMEA deals. SAP CRM (not SAP ERP) is a negative signal (25.6% win rate).
product_notes.DocAuto: Critical: <0.1% new logo win rate from cold prospecting. Best as expansion from existing Conga Composer customer or warm referral. Salesforce + document volume = high score. PDF Butler presence (in-flight) = 16% win rate — reassess viability. DocuSign/Adobe Sign renewal window = highest urgency signal.
product_notes.Aime: 87% Enterprise/Strategic. Requires large contract repository. Not greenfield. Best post-M&A or with existing CLM customer.
product_notes.RLM: Requires multiple revenue stream complexity. Best for SaaS, Telecom, FinServ, Rental. Only 12 deals in data — entirely an expansion motion. Mean ACV $119,951 — largest deals in portfolio when they close.
